text.div:
Narrativa gerada: StructureDefinition agrosus-ubs

NomeBandeirasCard.TipoDescrição e condicionalismos
Organization 0..*BRCoreOrganizationA grouping of people or organizations with a common purpose
Fatias para identifier Conteúdo/regras para todas as fatias
identifier:cnes S1..1IdentifierCódigo CNES da Unidade Básica de Saúde
identifier:cnpj S0..1IdentifierCNPJ da organização responsável pela UBS
active S1..1booleanIndica se a UBS está ativa
type S0..*CodeableConceptTipo do estabelecimento de saúde
name S1..1stringNome oficial da Unidade Básica de Saúde
telecom S0..*ContactPointMeios de contato da UBS
address S1..1AddressEndereço da UBS
partOf S0..1Reference(BRCoreOrganization)Secretaria, rede ou organização à qual a UBS pertence

Documentação para este formato

name: AgroSUSUBS
title: Unidade Básica de Saúde AgroSUS
description: Perfil da Unidade Básica de Saúde responsável pelo acompanhamento do trabalhador rural no AgroSUS.

element.short: A grouping of people or organizations with a common purpose
element.definition: A formally or informally recognized grouping of people or organizations formed for the purpose of achieving some form of collective action.  Includes companies, institutions, corporations, departments, community groups, healthcare practice groups, payer/insurer, etc.

constraint.human: If the resource is contained in another resource, it SHALL NOT contain nested Resources
If the resource is contained in another resource, it SHALL be referred to from elsewhere in the resource or SHALL refer to the containing resource
constraint.expression: contained.where((('#'+id in (%resource.descendants().reference | %resource.descendants().as(canonical) | %resource.descendants().as(uri) | %resource.descendants().as(url))) or descendants().where(reference = '#').exists() or descendants().where(as(canonical) = '#').exists() or descendants().where(as(canonical) = '#').exists()).not()).trace('unmatched', id).empty()
constraint.xpath: not(exists(for $id in f:contained/*/f:id/@value return $contained[not(parent::*/descendant::f:reference/@value=concat('#', $contained/*/id/@value) or descendant::f:reference[@value='#'])]))

constraint.human: If a resource is contained in another resource, it SHALL NOT have a meta.versionId or a meta.lastUpdated
If a resource is contained in another resource, it SHALL NOT have a security label

extension.valueMarkdown: When a resource has no narrative, only systems that fully understand the data can display the resource to a human safely. Including a human readable representation in the resource makes for a much more robust eco-system and cheaper handling of resources by intermediary systems. Some ecosystems restrict distribution of resources to only those systems that do fully understand the resources, and as a consequence implementers may believe that the narrative is superfluous. However experience shows that such eco-systems often open up to new participants over time.

constraint.human: A resource should have narrative for robust management
The organization SHALL at least have a name or an identifier, and possibly more than one

element.short: Identificador lógico deste artefato
element.definition: Identificador lógico deste artefato
element.comment: The only time that a resource does not have an id is when it is being submitted to the server using a create operation.

element.short: Metadados sobre o recurso
element.definition: Metadados sobre o recurso

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Um conjunto de regras sob as quais este conteúdo foi criado
element.definition: Um conjunto de regras sob as quais este conteúdo foi criado
element.comment: Asserting this rule set restricts the content to be only understood by a limited set of trading partners. This inherently limits the usefulness of the data in the long term. However, the existing health eco-system is highly fractured, and not yet ready to define, collect, and exchange data in a generally computable sense. Wherever possible, implementers and/or specification writers should avoid using this element. Often, when used, the URL is a reference to an implementation guide that defines these special rules as part of it's narrative along with other profiles, value sets, etc.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.isModifierReason: This element is labeled as a modifier because the implicit rules may provide additional knowledge about the resource that modifies it's meaning or interpretation

element.short: Idioma do recurso
element.definition: Idioma do recurso
element.comment: Language is provided to support indexing and accessibility (typically, services such as text to speech use the language tag). The html language tag in the narrative applies  to the narrative. The language tag on the resource may be used to specify the language of other presentations generated from the data in the resource. Not all the content has to be in the base language. The Resource.language should not be assumed to apply to the narrative automatically. If a language is specified, it should it also be specified on the div element in the html (see rules in HTML5 for information about the relationship between xml:lang and the html lang attribute).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Resumo do texto do recurso em linguagem natural
element.definition: Resumo do texto do recurso em linguagem natural
element.comment: Contained resources do not have narrative. Resources that are not contained SHOULD have a narrative. In some cases, a resource may only have text with little or no additional discrete data (as long as all minOccurs=1 elements are satisfied).  This may be necessary for data from legacy systems where information is captured as a "text blob" or where text is additionally entered raw or narrated and encoded information is added later.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Contained, inline Resources
element.definition: Recursos contidos no recurso
element.comment: This should never be done when the content can be identified properly, as once identification is lost, it is extremely difficult (and context dependent) to restore it again. Contained resources may have profiles and tags In their meta elements, but SHALL NOT have security labels.

element.short: Extension
element.definition: Extensões adicionais que não fazem parte do modelo base

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.short: Extensões adicionais que não podem ser ignoradas, mesmo que não reconhecidas
element.definition: Extensões adicionais que não podem ser ignoradas, mesmo que não reconhecidas

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.isModifierReason: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the resource that contains them

element.short: Identificadores externos para este recurso
element.definition: Identificadores externos para este recurso
element.requirements: Organizations are known by a variety of ids. Some institutions maintain several, and most collect identifiers for exchange with other organizations concerning the organization.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Código CNES da Unidade Básica de Saúde
element.definition: Identificador do estabelecimento
element.requirements: Organizations are known by a variety of ids. Some institutions maintain several, and most collect identifiers for exchange with other organizations concerning the organization.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Id lógico do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Id lógico do identificador do estabelecimento

slicing.description: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

element.short: Extensões do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Extensões incluídas no identificador do estabelecimento

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.short: Uso do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: usual: identificador usual do estabelecimento. official: identificador oficial do estabelecimento. temp: identificador temporário do estabelecimento. secondary: identificador secundário do estabelecimento
element.comment: Applications can assume that an identifier is permanent unless it explicitly says that it is temporary.
element.requirements: Allows the appropriate identifier for a particular context of use to be selected from among a set of identifiers.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.isModifierReason: This is labeled as "Is Modifier" because applications should not mistake a temporary id for a permanent one.

binding.description: Identifies the purpose for this identifier, if known .

element.short: Tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Código que representa o tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.comment: This element deals only with general categories of identifiers.  It SHOULD not be used for codes that correspond 1..1 with the Identifier.system. Some identifiers may fall into multiple categories due to common usage.   Where the system is known, a type is unnecessary because the type is always part of the system definition. However systems often need to handle identifiers where the system is not known. There is not a 1:1 relationship between type and system, since many different systems have the same type.
element.requirements: Allows users to make use of identifiers when the identifier system is not known.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Id lógico do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Id lógico do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento

slicing.description: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

element.short: Extensões do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Extensões incluídas no tipo do identificador do estabelecimento

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.short: Codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Codificação que representa o tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.comment: Codes may be defined very casually in enumerations, or code lists, up to very formal definitions such as SNOMED CT - see the HL7 v3 Core Principles for more information.  Ordering of codings is undefined and SHALL NOT be used to infer meaning. Generally, at most only one of the coding values will be labeled as UserSelected = true.
element.requirements: Allows for alternative encodings within a code system, and translations to other code systems.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Id lógico da codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Id lógico da codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento

slicing.description: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

element.short: Extensões da codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Extensões incluídas na codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.short: Sistema da codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Sistema que identifica a codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.comment: The URI may be an OID (urn:oid:...) or a UUID (urn:uuid:...).  OIDs and UUIDs SHALL be references to the HL7 OID registry. Otherwise, the URI should come from HL7's list of FHIR defined special URIs or it should reference to some definition that establishes the system clearly and unambiguously.
element.requirements: Need to be unambiguous about the source of the definition of the symbol.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Versão da codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Versão da codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.comment: Where the terminology does not clearly define what string should be used to identify code system versions, the recommendation is to use the date (expressed in FHIR date format) on which that version was officially published as the version date.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Código da codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Código que representa a codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.requirements: Need to refer to a particular code in the system.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Texto da codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Texto que representa a codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.requirements: Need to be able to carry a human-readable meaning of the code for readers that do not know  the system.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: C*E.2 - but note this is not well followed

element.short: Seleção do usuário da codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Indica se a codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento foi selecionada pelo usuário
element.comment: Amongst a set of alternatives, a directly chosen code is the most appropriate starting point for new translations. There is some ambiguity about what exactly 'directly chosen' implies, and trading partner agreement may be needed to clarify the use of this element and its consequences more completely.
element.requirements: This has been identified as a clinical safety criterium - that this exact system/code pair was chosen explicitly, rather than inferred by the system based on some rules or language processing.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: fhir:Coding.userSelected fhir:mapsTo dt:CDCoding.codingRationale. fhir:Coding.userSelected fhir:hasMap fhir:Coding.userSelected.map. fhir:Coding.userSelected.map a fhir:Map;   fhir:target dt:CDCoding.codingRationale. fhir:Coding.userSelected\#true a [     fhir:source "true";     fhir:target dt:CDCoding.codingRationale\#O   ]

element.short: Texto do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Texto que representa o tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.comment: Very often the text is the same as a displayName of one of the codings.
element.requirements: The codes from the terminologies do not always capture the correct meaning with all the nuances of the human using them, or sometimes there is no appropriate code at all. In these cases, the text is used to capture the full meaning of the source.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: C*E.9. But note many systems use C*E.2 for this

element.short: Sistema do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Sistema que identifica o tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.comment: Identifier.system is always case sensitive.
element.requirements: There are many sets  of identifiers.  To perform matching of two identifiers, we need to know what set we're dealing with. The system identifies a particular set of unique identifiers.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Valor do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Valor do identificador do estabelecimento
element.comment: If the value is a full URI, then the system SHALL be urn:ietf:rfc:3986.  The value's primary purpose is computational mapping.  As a result, it may be normalized for comparison purposes (e.g. removing non-significant whitespace, dashes, etc.)  A value formatted for human display can be conveyed using the [Rendered Value extension](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/extension-rendered-value.html). Identifier.value is to be treated as case sensitive unless knowledge of the Identifier.system allows the processer to be confident that non-case-sensitive processing is safe.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: II.extension or II.root if system indicates OID or GUID (Or Role.id.extension or root)

element.short: Período de uso do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Período de tempo durante o qual o identificador do estabelecimento foi utilizado

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Referência à organização que atribuiu o identificador ao estabelecimento
element.definition: Referência à organização que atribuiu o identificador ao estabelecimento
element.comment: The Identifier.assigner may omit the .reference element and only contain a .display element reflecting the name or other textual information about the assigning organization.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: II.assigningAuthorityName but note that this is an improper use by the definition of the field.  Also Role.scoper

element.short: Identificador único para referência cruzada
element.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

slicing.description: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

element.short: Extensões adicionais definidas pela implementação
element.definition: Extensões adicionais definidas pela implementação

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.short: Referência literal, URL relativa, interna ou absoluta
element.definition: Referência literal, URL relativa, interna ou absoluta
element.comment: Using absolute URLs provides a stable scalable approach suitable for a cloud/web context, while using relative/logical references provides a flexible approach suitable for use when trading across closed eco-system boundaries.   Absolute URLs do not need to point to a FHIR RESTful server, though this is the preferred approach. If the URL conforms to the structure "/[type]/[id]" then it should be assumed that the reference is to a FHIR RESTful server.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Tipo referenciado (p. ex. br-core-patient)
element.definition: Tipo referenciado (p. ex. br-core-patient)
element.comment: This element is used to indicate the type of  the target of the reference. This may be used which ever of the other elements are populated (or not). In some cases, the type of the target may be determined by inspection of the reference (e.g. a RESTful URL) or by resolving the target of the reference; if both the type and a reference is provided, the reference SHALL resolve to a resource of the same type as that specified.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

binding.description: Aa resource (or, for logical models, the URI of the logical model).

element.short: Referência lógica, quando a referência literal não é conhecida
element.definition: Referência lógica, quando a referência literal não é conhecida
element.comment:
When an identifier is provided in place of a reference, any system processing the reference will only be able to resolve the identifier to a reference if it understands the business context in which the identifier is used. Sometimes this is global (e.g. a national identifier) but often it is not. For this reason, none of the useful mechanisms described for working with references (e.g. chaining, includes) are possible, nor should servers be expected to be able resolve the reference. Servers may accept an identifier based reference untouched, resolve it, and/or reject it - see CapabilityStatement.rest.resource.referencePolicy. 

When both an identifier and a literal reference are provided, the literal reference is preferred. Applications processing the resource are allowed - but not required - to check that the identifier matches the literal reference

Applications converting a logical reference to a literal reference may choose to leave the logical reference present, or remove it.

Reference is intended to point to a structure that can potentially be expressed as a FHIR resource, though there is no need for it to exist as an actual FHIR resource instance - except in as much as an application wishes to actual find the target of the reference. The content referred to be the identifier must meet the logical constraints implied by any limitations on what resource types are permitted for the reference.  For example, it would not be legitimate to send the identifier for a drug prescription if the type were Reference(Observation|DiagnosticReport).  One of the use-cases for Reference.identifier is the situation where no FHIR representation exists (where the type is Reference (Any).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Nome da entidade que atribuiu o identificador da organização
element.definition: Nome da entidade que atribuiu o identificador da organização
element.comment: This is generally not the same as the Resource.text of the referenced resource.  The purpose is to identify what's being referenced, not to fully describe it.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.comment: Using absolute URLs provides a stable scalable approach suitable for a cloud/web context, while using relative/logical references provides a flexible approach suitable for use when trading across closed eco-system boundaries.   Absolute URLs do not need to point to a FHIR RESTful server, though this is the preferred approach. If the URL conforms to the structure "/[type]/[id]" then it should be assumed that the reference is to a FHIR RESTful server.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Tipo referenciado (p. ex. br-core-patient)
element.definition: Tipo referenciado (p. ex. br-core-patient)
element.comment: This element is used to indicate the type of  the target of the reference. This may be used which ever of the other elements are populated (or not). In some cases, the type of the target may be determined by inspection of the reference (e.g. a RESTful URL) or by resolving the target of the reference; if both the type and a reference is provided, the reference SHALL resolve to a resource of the same type as that specified.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

binding.description: Aa resource (or, for logical models, the URI of the logical model).

element.short: Referência lógica, quando a referência literal não é conhecida
element.definition: Referência lógica, quando a referência literal não é conhecida
element.comment:
When an identifier is provided in place of a reference, any system processing the reference will only be able to resolve the identifier to a reference if it understands the business context in which the identifier is used. Sometimes this is global (e.g. a national identifier) but often it is not. For this reason, none of the useful mechanisms described for working with references (e.g. chaining, includes) are possible, nor should servers be expected to be able resolve the reference. Servers may accept an identifier based reference untouched, resolve it, and/or reject it - see CapabilityStatement.rest.resource.referencePolicy. 

When both an identifier and a literal reference are provided, the literal reference is preferred. Applications processing the resource are allowed - but not required - to check that the identifier matches the literal reference

Applications converting a logical reference to a literal reference may choose to leave the logical reference present, or remove it.

Reference is intended to point to a structure that can potentially be expressed as a FHIR resource, though there is no need for it to exist as an actual FHIR resource instance - except in as much as an application wishes to actual find the target of the reference. The content referred to be the identifier must meet the logical constraints implied by any limitations on what resource types are permitted for the reference.  For example, it would not be legitimate to send the identifier for a drug prescription if the type were Reference(Observation|DiagnosticReport).  One of the use-cases for Reference.identifier is the situation where no FHIR representation exists (where the type is Reference (Any).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Nome da entidade que atribuiu o identificador da organização
element.definition: Nome da entidade que atribuiu o identificador da organização
element.comment: This is generally not the same as the Resource.text of the referenced resource.  The purpose is to identify what's being referenced, not to fully describe it.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: CNPJ da organização responsável pela UBS
element.definition: Identificador do estabelecimento
element.requirements: Organizations are known by a variety of ids. Some institutions maintain several, and most collect identifiers for exchange with other organizations concerning the organization.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Id lógico do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Id lógico do identificador do estabelecimento

slicing.description: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

element.short: Extensões do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Extensões incluídas no identificador do estabelecimento

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.short: Uso do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: usual: identificador usual do estabelecimento. official: identificador oficial do estabelecimento. temp: identificador temporário do estabelecimento. secondary: identificador secundário do estabelecimento
element.comment: Applications can assume that an identifier is permanent unless it explicitly says that it is temporary.
element.requirements: Allows the appropriate identifier for a particular context of use to be selected from among a set of identifiers.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.isModifierReason: This is labeled as "Is Modifier" because applications should not mistake a temporary id for a permanent one.

binding.description: Identifies the purpose for this identifier, if known .

element.short: Tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Código que representa o tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.comment: This element deals only with general categories of identifiers.  It SHOULD not be used for codes that correspond 1..1 with the Identifier.system. Some identifiers may fall into multiple categories due to common usage.   Where the system is known, a type is unnecessary because the type is always part of the system definition. However systems often need to handle identifiers where the system is not known. There is not a 1:1 relationship between type and system, since many different systems have the same type.
element.requirements: Allows users to make use of identifiers when the identifier system is not known.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Id lógico do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Id lógico do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento

slicing.description: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

element.short: Extensões do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Extensões incluídas no tipo do identificador do estabelecimento

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.short: Codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Codificação que representa o tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.comment: Codes may be defined very casually in enumerations, or code lists, up to very formal definitions such as SNOMED CT - see the HL7 v3 Core Principles for more information.  Ordering of codings is undefined and SHALL NOT be used to infer meaning. Generally, at most only one of the coding values will be labeled as UserSelected = true.
element.requirements: Allows for alternative encodings within a code system, and translations to other code systems.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Id lógico da codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Id lógico da codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento

slicing.description: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

element.short: Extensões da codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Extensões incluídas na codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.short: Sistema da codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Sistema que identifica a codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.comment: The URI may be an OID (urn:oid:...) or a UUID (urn:uuid:...).  OIDs and UUIDs SHALL be references to the HL7 OID registry. Otherwise, the URI should come from HL7's list of FHIR defined special URIs or it should reference to some definition that establishes the system clearly and unambiguously.
element.requirements: Need to be unambiguous about the source of the definition of the symbol.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Versão da codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Versão da codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.comment: Where the terminology does not clearly define what string should be used to identify code system versions, the recommendation is to use the date (expressed in FHIR date format) on which that version was officially published as the version date.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Código da codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Código que representa a codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.requirements: Need to refer to a particular code in the system.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Texto da codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Texto que representa a codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.requirements: Need to be able to carry a human-readable meaning of the code for readers that do not know  the system.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: C*E.2 - but note this is not well followed

element.short: Seleção do usuário da codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Indica se a codificação do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento foi selecionada pelo usuário
element.comment: Amongst a set of alternatives, a directly chosen code is the most appropriate starting point for new translations. There is some ambiguity about what exactly 'directly chosen' implies, and trading partner agreement may be needed to clarify the use of this element and its consequences more completely.
element.requirements: This has been identified as a clinical safety criterium - that this exact system/code pair was chosen explicitly, rather than inferred by the system based on some rules or language processing.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: fhir:Coding.userSelected fhir:mapsTo dt:CDCoding.codingRationale. fhir:Coding.userSelected fhir:hasMap fhir:Coding.userSelected.map. fhir:Coding.userSelected.map a fhir:Map;   fhir:target dt:CDCoding.codingRationale. fhir:Coding.userSelected\#true a [     fhir:source "true";     fhir:target dt:CDCoding.codingRationale\#O   ]

element.short: Texto do tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Texto que representa o tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.comment: Very often the text is the same as a displayName of one of the codings.
element.requirements: The codes from the terminologies do not always capture the correct meaning with all the nuances of the human using them, or sometimes there is no appropriate code at all. In these cases, the text is used to capture the full meaning of the source.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: C*E.9. But note many systems use C*E.2 for this

element.short: Sistema do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Sistema que identifica o tipo do identificador do estabelecimento
element.comment: Identifier.system is always case sensitive.
element.requirements: There are many sets  of identifiers.  To perform matching of two identifiers, we need to know what set we're dealing with. The system identifies a particular set of unique identifiers.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Valor do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Valor do identificador do estabelecimento
element.comment: If the value is a full URI, then the system SHALL be urn:ietf:rfc:3986.  The value's primary purpose is computational mapping.  As a result, it may be normalized for comparison purposes (e.g. removing non-significant whitespace, dashes, etc.)  A value formatted for human display can be conveyed using the [Rendered Value extension](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/extension-rendered-value.html). Identifier.value is to be treated as case sensitive unless knowledge of the Identifier.system allows the processer to be confident that non-case-sensitive processing is safe.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: II.extension or II.root if system indicates OID or GUID (Or Role.id.extension or root)

element.short: Período de uso do identificador do estabelecimento
element.definition: Período de tempo durante o qual o identificador do estabelecimento foi utilizado

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Referência à organização que atribuiu o identificador ao estabelecimento
element.definition: Referência à organização que atribuiu o identificador ao estabelecimento
element.comment: The Identifier.assigner may omit the .reference element and only contain a .display element reflecting the name or other textual information about the assigning organization.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: II.assigningAuthorityName but note that this is an improper use by the definition of the field.  Also Role.scoper

element.short: Identificador único para referência cruzada
element.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

element.definition: Extensões adicionais definidas pela implementação

element.comment:
When an identifier is provided in place of a reference, any system processing the reference will only be able to resolve the identifier to a reference if it understands the business context in which the identifier is used. Sometimes this is global (e.g. a national identifier) but often it is not. For this reason, none of the useful mechanisms described for working with references (e.g. chaining, includes) are possible, nor should servers be expected to be able resolve the reference. Servers may accept an identifier based reference untouched, resolve it, and/or reject it - see CapabilityStatement.rest.resource.referencePolicy.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Nome da entidade que atribuiu o identificador da organização
element.definition: Nome da entidade que atribuiu o identificador da organização
element.comment: This is generally not the same as the Resource.text of the referenced resource.  The purpose is to identify what's being referenced, not to fully describe it.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Indica se a UBS está ativa
element.definition: Indica se a Organização está ativa
element.comment:
This active flag is not intended to be used to mark an organization as temporarily closed or under construction. Instead the Location(s) within the Organization should have the suspended status. If further details of the reason for the suspension are required, then an extension on this element should be used.

This element is labeled as a modifier because it may be used to mark that the resource was created in error.
element.requirements: Need a flag to indicate a record is no longer to be used and should generally be hidden for the user in the UI.
element.meaningWhenMissing: This resource is generally assumed to be active if no value is provided for the active element

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.isModifierReason: This element is labelled as a modifier because it is a status element that can indicate that a record should not be treated as valid

mapping.map: ./Status (however this concept in ServD more covers why the organization is active or not, could be delisted, deregistered, not operational yet) this could alternatively be derived from ./StartDate and ./EndDate and given a context date.

element.short: Tipo do estabelecimento de saúde
element.definition: Tipo da organização
element.comment:
Organizations can be corporations, wards, sections, clinical teams, government departments, etc. Note that code is generally a classifier of the type of organization; in many applications, codes are used to identity a particular organization (say, ward) as opposed to another of the same type - these are identifiers, not codes

When considering if multiple types are appropriate, you should evaluate if child organizations would be a more appropriate use of the concept, as different types likely are in different sub-areas of the organization. This is most likely to be used where type values have orthogonal values, such as a religious, academic and medical center.

We expect that some jurisdictions will profile this optionality to be a single cardinality.
element.requirements: Need to be able to track the kind of organization that this is - different organization types have different uses.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Nome oficial da Unidade Básica de Saúde
element.definition: Nome da organização
element.comment: If the name of an organization changes, consider putting the old name in the alias column so that it can still be located through searches.
element.requirements: Need to use the name as the label of the organization.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Razão social da organização
element.definition: Razão social da organização
element.comment: There are no dates associated with the alias/historic names, as this is not intended to track when names were used, but to assist in searching so that older names can still result in identifying the organization.
element.requirements:
Over time locations and organizations go through many changes and can be known by different names.

For searching knowing previous names that the organization was known by can be very useful.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Meios de contato da UBS
element.definition: Meio de contato
element.comment: The use code 'home' is not to be used. Note that these contacts are not the contact details of people who are employed by or represent the organization, but official contacts for the organization itself.
element.requirements: Human contact for the organization.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
The telecom of an organization can never be of use 'home'

element.short: Endereço da UBS
element.definition: Endereço
element.comment: Organization may have multiple addresses with different uses or applicable periods. The use code 'home' is not to be used.
element.requirements: May need to keep track of the organization's addresses for contacting, billing or reporting requirements.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
An address of an organization can never be of use 'home'

element.short: Secretaria, rede ou organização à qual a UBS pertence
element.definition: Referência à Organização associada (p.ex. Mantenedora)
element.requirements: Need to be able to track the hierarchy of organizations within an organization.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Contato
element.definition: Contato para a organização para uma determinada finalidade
element.comment: Where multiple contacts for the same purpose are provided there is a standard extension that can be used to determine which one is the preferred contact to use.
element.requirements: Need to keep track of assigned contact points within bigger organization.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Identificação unívoca da pessoa de contato
element.definition: Identificação unívoca da pessoa de contato

element.definition: Extensões adicionais que não fazem parte do modelo base

Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.isModifierReason: Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them

element.short: Tipo de Contato
element.definition: Tipo de Contato
element.requirements: Need to distinguish between multiple contact persons.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

binding.description: The purpose for which you would contact a contact party.

element.short: Nome associado ao contato
element.definition: Nome associado ao contato
element.requirements: Need to be able to track the person by name.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Meio de Contato
element.definition: Meio de Contato
element.requirements: People have (primary) ways to contact them in some way such as phone, email.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Endereço do contato
element.definition: Endereço do contato
element.requirements: May need to keep track of a contact party's address for contacting, billing or reporting requirements.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Endpoints técnicos que fornecem acesso a serviços eletrônicos operados pela organização
element.definition: Endpoints técnicos que fornecem acesso a serviços eletrônicos operados pela organização
element.requirements: Organizations have multiple systems that provide various services and need to be able to define the technical connection details for how to connect to them, and for what purpose.
